stepAnswer: When you know two angles and one side (AAS or ASA), or two sides and a $$non-included$$ angle (SSA).
stepTitle: Under which conditions can the Law of Sines be used to find a missing angle?
choices: When you know two angles and one side (AAS or ASA), or two sides and a $$non-included$$ angle (SSA).
When you know two sides and the included angle (SAS), or all three sides (SSS).
Only when the triangle is a right triangle.
When you know all three angles of the triangle.
